attack_surface.exposure: All Json/Text/Plang/PlangData serializer methods now return Data<T> with typed errors instead of throwing
attack_surface.trust_boundary: wire ingress through channels; settings sqlite store; application/plang transport
mitigations: Catch list narrowed but covers JsonException/NotSupportedException/IOException — STJ's primary failure modes
application/plang transport preserves Signature inflow via _transportInOptions (explicit comment added)
Envelope deserialization still defers trust to downstream VerifyAsync
gaps: OperationCanceledException and EndOfStreamException from serializer not in filter; bubble past Channel.ReadAsT (see F3)

attack_surface.exposure: ReadLimitedBytesAsync collapsed from two copies into one; new Response record dispatches body shape by Content-Type
mitigations: Slow-loris guard: 1KB/s minimum over 30s window, typed SlowResponse 408
TextFallback only stringifies bytes the body was already capped on

attack_surface.exposure: New IClass.Build() invoked at validate; runs handler code over .pr params with __action/__app stamped via SetAction
mitigations: file.read.Build() ExistsAsync() probe wraps in catch-all (silent best-effort)
AuthGate denials swallowed by the catch — no info disclosure
Build() Ok() default impl means handlers opt in

attack_surface.exposure: Channels.Channel(name) returns process-wide __noop__ sink when channel missing (silent drop)
mitigations: Distinct from Resolve(name) which returns null and requires explicit handling
Today's only callers are advisory build-time writes whose silent-drop is intentional
gaps: If audit/security channels are introduced and a caller uses the convenience accessor, missing channel would swallow events (see F1)

findings.vector: Channels.Channel(name) is silent-drop by design — process-wide __noop__ sink returned on miss. If an audit/security/trace channel is ever added and a caller writes through the convenience accessor instead of Resolve+null-handling, missing the channel registration would silently swallow events.
findings.preconditions: A future channel with security semantics + a caller using Channels.Channel(name) instead of Resolve
findings.impact: Lost audit trail / silent failure of a security-relevant write path
findings.exploit_sketch: n/a today — no audit channel exists. Latent design risk.
findings.proposed_fix: Strengthen xmldoc on Channels.Channel(name): "do not use for channels whose absence is a security signal — use Resolve and handle null." When the next security-channel ships, route writers through Resolve.

findings.vector: New Data.As(string typeName) resolves an arbitrary PLang type name through the registry and coerces. If a future caller threads externally-sourced typeName, downstream code assuming a shape could see a wrong CLR type.
findings.preconditions: Caller passes attacker-influenced typeName without allowlist validation
findings.impact: Runtime type confusion at the call site (no current path; informational)
findings.exploit_sketch: n/a — no current caller passes untrusted typeName. Currently used only with build-time inferred destination extension.
findings.proposed_fix: Strengthen xmldoc on Data.As(string): "Caller MUST own typeName; never thread an externally-sourced string through here without validating against a closed allowlist." Consider an internal allowlist param if a wire-facing caller ever needs this.

findings.vector: Channels.ReadAsT<T> removed its outer catch-all in favour of forwarding the serializer's Data<T> directly. Serializers catch JsonException/NotSupportedException/IOException, but OperationCanceledException and EndOfStreamException now bubble up as raw exceptions instead of typed ReadError.
findings.preconditions: Cancellation token fires during channel read, or stream truncates without raising IOException
findings.impact: Layered containment lost; raw exception escapes ReadAsT into the action runner (which has its own catch). User-facing error stays typed via the source-gen ExecuteAsync wrapper. Net regression is one of defense-in-depth, not bypass.
findings.exploit_sketch: Cancel a long-running channel read; observe stack-trace path differs from prior typed-error path.
findings.proposed_fix: Restore the outer try/catch in ReadAsT (cheap defense-in-depth) OR add OperationCanceledException to each serializer's filter.

summary: Wide structural typed-return diff with no new critical/high. HTTP size-cap consolidation closes a prior audit-list duplicate-impl concern (net security improvement). AuthGate canonicalization (purge-systemio v2) holds intact. Three low/info opens, none merge-blocking.
